children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Every vessel required to make entry under or obtain clearance under shall have a manifest that complies with the requirements prescribed under subsection (d).section 1434 of this titlesection 60105 of title 46

children.heading: Production of manifest
children.label: b Production of manifest
children.text: Any manifest required by the Customs Service shall be signed, produced, delivered or electronically transmitted by the master or person in charge of the vessel, aircraft, or vehicle, or by any other authorized agent of the owner or operator of the vessel, aircraft, or vehicle in accordance with the requirements prescribed under subsection (d). A manifest may be supplemented by bill of lading data supplied by the issuer of such bill. If any irregularity of omission or commission occurs in any way in respect to any manifest or bill of lading data, the owner or operator of the vessel, aircraft or vehicle, or any party responsible for such irregularity, shall be liable for any fine or penalty prescribed by law with respect to such irregularity. The Customs Service may take appropriate action against any of the parties.

children.heading: Public disclosure of certain manifest information
children.label: c Public disclosure of certain manifest information
A
children.text: The name and address of each importer or consignee and the name and address of the shipper to such importer or consignee, unless the importer or consignee has made a biennial certification, in accordance with procedures adopted by the Secretary of the Treasury, claiming confidential treatment of such information.

children.label: B
children.text: The general character of the cargo.

children.label: C
children.text: The number of packages and gross weight.

children.label: D
children.text: The name of the vessel, aircraft, or carrier.

children.label: E
children.text: The seaport or airport of loading.

children.label: F
children.text: The seaport or airport of discharge.

children.label: G
children.text: The country of origin of the shipment.

children.label: H
children.text: The trademarks appearing on the goods or packages.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary of the Treasury makes an affirmative finding on a shipment-by-shipment basis that disclosure is likely to pose a threat of personal injury or property damage; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the information is exempt under the provisions of .section 552(b)(1) of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that any personally identifiable information, including Social Security numbers and passport numbers, is removed from any manifest signed, produced, delivered, or electronically transmitted under this section before access to the manifest is provided to the public.

The Secretary of the Treasury, in order to allow for the timely dissemination and publication of the information listed in paragraph (1), shall establish procedures to provide access to manifests. Such procedures shall include provisions for adequate protection against the public disclosure of information not available for public disclosure from such manifests.

children.label: A
children.text: specify the form for, and the information and data that must be contained in, the manifest required by subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: allow, at the option of the individual producing the manifest and subject to paragraph (2), letters and documents shipments to be accounted for by summary manifesting procedures;

children.label: C
children.text: prescribe the manner of production for, and the delivery for electronic transmittal of, the manifest required by subsection (a); and

children.label: D
children.text: prescribe the manner for supplementing manifests with bill of lading data under subsection (b).

children.label: i
children.text: that they be segregated by country of origin, and

children.label: ii
children.text: additional examination procedures that are not necessary for individually manifested shipments;

children.label: B
children.text: standard letter envelopes and standard document packs shall be segregated from larger document shipments for purposes of customs inspections; and

children.label: i
children.text: data described in General Headnote 4(c) of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States,

children.label: ii
children.text: securities and similar evidences of value described in heading 4907 of such Schedule, but not monetary instruments defined pursuant to chapter 53 of title 31, and

children.label: iii
children.text: personal correspondence, whether on paper, cards, photographs, tapes, or other media.
